title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Language Clinic: Stdout Stderr Contract
summary: The report encodes meaning only with color, clips long words, changes columns by terminal width, mixes diagnostics with JSON stdout, and gives screen-reader users an unlabeled number grid. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stdout stderr contract increment in accessible human and machine report system. Lear…
objectives: Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_report_format_accessibility_3(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-stdout-stderr-contract-uo6y8d
# Competency: bookbot-stdout-stderr-contract.
# Case 3804: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_stdout_stderr_contract_uo6y8d(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_uo6y8d = "uo6y8d-d8y6ou-3804"
    assert evidence_variant_uo6y8d
    evidence_axes_uo6y8d = (
        "corpusuo6y8d",
        "policyd8y6ou",
        "revision3804",
        "commanduo6y8d3804",
        "reportd8y6ou3804",
        "test3804uo6y8d",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_uo6y8d)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"
